rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION On The Administrator And Respondent Thao Thi Lan Nguyen Petition For Order For Reimbursement
rulings.Rulings: Respondent Son Nguyen's Motion for Summary Adjudication of Administrator's Petition for Order for Reimbursement is denied. "A motion for summary adjudication shall be granted only if it completely disposes of a cause of action, an affirmative defense, a claim for damages, or an issue of duty." (CCP § 437c(f)(1).) None of the six issues raised in the Motion, taken individually as the Court must, would completely dispose of the contribution action the Administrator brings in the underlying petition under Probate Code § 9651. At most, if successful, each issue would reduce or place limits on or enable offsets to the amount of contribution claimed. Petitioner Thao Thi Lan Nguyen failed to present any evidence in support of her opposition to this motion and failed to file the required response to Respondent's Separate Statement of Undisputed Material Facts. Therefore, Respondent is correct that Petitioner has not raised triable issues of material fact, and the Court accepts Respondent's relevant, supported facts as true for purposes of this motion. Nonetheless, Respondent must still carry his initial burden of showing that Petitioner cannot prevail. "A defendant […] has met his or her burden of showing that a cause of action has no merit if the party has shown that one or more elements of the cause of action, even if not separately pleaded, cannot be established, or that there is a complete defense to the cause of action." (CCP § 437c(p)(2)(emphasis added.) Here, in addition to failing to completely dispose of the claim, there is no evidence submitted that establishes either the alleged waiver or oral contract between the parties regarding contribution to the real property's expenses. Rejection of an offer to contribute at a time when Petitioner believed she was a sole owner, standing alone, does not establish knowing waiver of a right to later claim contribution once her legal position was rejected (especially where accepting the offer might have undercut her legal position), nor does it create an oral contract never to claim contribution. Even assuming that the statute of limitations would bar claims for contribution prior to March 2, 2015, there is no evidence presented as to which specific claims pre-dating March 2, 2015 are allegedly barred by the statute of limitations. Again in addition to failing to dispose of an entire claim, there is no evidence presented establishing the exact amount of any contribution obligation or the exact amount of any offsets. As already stated in the Court's May 23, 2019 Order granting in part and denying in part Petitioner's motion for summary adjudication of his Petition for Enforcement of Judgment, the Administrator's Petition for Reimbursement has not yet been heard or determined, and the ultimate determination of how to balance the right to recover and offsets will depend on facts not all of which are determined yet, the application of relevant law, and the exercise of the Court's equitable discretion. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. Wednesday, September 18, 2019.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MAGILAVY FAMILY TRUST ESTABLISHED JUNE 5, 1989 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order That Petitioner Requests For Admission, Set 9 Be Deemed Admitted And For Monetary Sanction
rulings.Rulings: The motion is granted, no opposition having been filed and no substantially compliant response to the requests for admission having been served prior to the hearing date. The requests for admission, to the extent they are within the scope of CCP section 2033.010, are deemed admitted, and Ms. Magilavy is ordered to pay Ms. Thomas sixty dollars ($60) as sanctions. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. Wednesday, September 18, 2019.
